subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Overall narrative is coherent and logically developed
Clear numbering of major headings and placement of figures close to relevant text
Exploration stays within the word limit and covers all key sections
weaknesses: Abrupt transitions between some sections (e.g., LCM discussion to simultaneous equations)
Inconsistent figure and heading numbering and formatting
Repetition of algebraic steps leads to unnecessary length
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments point to a clear and logically developed structure with only minor lapses. Organization is solid, but not exemplary, and conciseness is mostly achieved despite some repetition. These features align with a band-3 performance (well organized and coherent but not exceptional).

strengths: Use of standard mathematical notation (Î¸, subscripts, Ï) throughout
Inclusion of unit-circle sketches, sine-wave graphs, and external reference plots
Most algebraic explanations precede the manipulations
weaknesses: Some notation inconsistencies (calling 365 and 687 the 'lowest common factor', inconsistent boldface)
Axes and scales are missing in graphs; variables defined after first use
Transitions between general solutions and numerical results are terse
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work uses appropriate mathematical language and multiple representations to some extent, but notation errors and clarity issues limit effectiveness. Logical structure is present but only to a moderate level, corresponding to band-2 performance.

strengths: Opening anecdote provides a brief personal voice
Choice of EarthâMars model shows some initiative
Use of colour-coded graphs reflects minimal personal approach
weaknesses: Mathematics largely follows a standard textbook derivation
No novel techniques or extensions are attempted
Limited independent thinking beyond selecting the planetary case
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evidence of personal engagement is minimal: a short anecdote and simple choice of example. There is limited personal approach, creativity, or independent thinking, matching band-1 descriptors.

strengths: Basic comparison of calculated vs. theoretical synodic period
Reporting of percentage error provides elementary evaluation
weaknesses: No discussion of model limitations or error sources
Lacks depth in critical analysis and no suggestions for future work
Reflection confined to noting numerical closeness without insight
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present at a basic level but lacks depth, critical analysis, and comprehensive evaluation. It aligns with band-1, where only limited reflection and evaluation occur.

strengths: Trigonometric model directly addresses the exploration question
Mathematics is at the appropriate SL level (trig identities, LCM, period conversion)
Derivations are generally correct and relevant
weaknesses: Mislabeling the LCM as 'lowest common factor'
Conceptual slips in rounding and no domain specified for integer k
Scope is narrow (circular orbits only), with missed opportunities for extension
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematics is relevant and mostly at course level with reasonable accuracy, but conceptual errors and limited scope restrict performance to band-2. The work shows some understanding and application but not comprehensive or expert usage.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations usually precede algebra, but the transition from derived general solutions to numerical period is terse, and variables (E_t, M_t) are defined after use.  These issues give only âsome logical structure and clarity,â matching band 2.

Derivations are generally correct but include conceptual slips (calling the product the âlowest common factorâ; rounding 778.74 then using 778.84 later).  These errors indicate partial understanding, consistent with band 2.

The student supplies unit-circle sketches, a multicolour sine-wave graph and a reference plot from literature, providing âsomeâ alternative representations.  Axes lack labels and scales, and no table of calculated values is included, so effectiveness is limited â band 2.

The investigation follows a recognisable sequence (introduction â formulation â solution â discussion â conclusion) and arguments progress in a way that the reader can follow without back-tracking.  Minor lapses occur (e.g., the jump from the LCM discussion to simultaneous equations is abrupt and one paragraph of algebra is repeated almost verbatim), yet overall the narrative is logical.  These features indicate clear, but not exemplary, coherence and development, best matched to band 3.

Using colour-coded graphs shows a small measure of initiative, yet no novel mathematical technique or creative extension (e.g., non-circular orbits) is attempted.  Hence the strand sits at the lower end, band 1.

A basic comparison between calculated and theoretical synodic periods constitutes elementary analysis.  No exploration of why the two values coincide or of model limitations is provided.  This is characteristic of band 1.
Evaluation is restricted to quoting a 0.142 % difference; there is no discussion of error sources, robustness, or future work.  Such minimal evaluation aligns with band 1.

Reflection is confined to noting the modelâs result is âremarkably closeâ to the textbook formula and mentioning percentage error.  There is recognition but little depth, so the work shows only âlimited reflectionâ â band 1.

Standard notation (Î¸, subscripts, Ï) and function syntax are used, yet misconceptions such as calling 365 and 687 the âlowest common factorâ and inconsistent boldface reduce accuracy.  Hence âsome appropriateâ language rather than âmostly consistentâ use â band 2.

The personal voice appears briefly in the opening anecdote, but the mathematics is presented impersonally and the approach mirrors the standard synodic-period derivation.  This satisfies only âsome evidenceâ of a personal approach â band 1.

At â1 900 words the exploration stays within the limit, but several explanatory paragraphs restate identical algebra and the introduction digresses into planetary-alignment trivia that is never revisited.  While most material is relevant, this repetition shows the piece is only "mostly" concise, corresponding to band 2.

The trigonometric model is clearly relevant, yet the mathematics is narrow in scope and excludes any dynamic or non-circular considerations.  This represents âsome relevant mathematics with limited application,â placing it in band 2.

Independent thinking is limited to choosing Mars/Earth and deciding to model with circular orbits; thereafter the work follows textbook trigonometric identities.  Evidence is therefore âlimited,â corresponding to band 1.

Headings are numbered and ordered; figures are placed close to the text that references them; the reader can quickly locate each stage of the solution.  However, figure numbering is inconsistent (two figures labelled âFigure 3â), and some captions are generic.  These weaknesses reduce the work from an âexceptionalâ structure to a solid, well-organised one, aligning with band 3.

Solving simultaneous trig equations, using LCM, and converting periods are broadly at SL level; no HL topics (e.g., differential equations) appear.  Thus the mathematics is âmostly at course level,â best fitting band 2.

annotations.comment: The title is clear and indicates the investigation topic effectively, but consider specifying the synodic period focus to enhance precision.
The word count and page count are administrative details and do not contribute to the academic content; they could be moved to an appendix or removed.
The opening anecdote is engaging but digresses into planetary trivia never referenced later; focus strictly on model rationale to maintain relevance.
The definition of âtotal planetary alignmentâ is explicit and well-placed, providing clear scope for the investigation.
The list of model assumptions is concise and clear; however, grouping them into numbered subsections would improve structure.
Figure 1 lacks axis labels and scale markers; include x- and y-axis labels on the unit circle diagram to clarify coordinate definitions.
Assumptions list is thorough, but adding a table summarizing these assumptions could enhance readability and quick reference.
The transition from model description to introducing functions in Solution 3 is abrupt; insert a brief bridging paragraph to improve coherence.
Notation in equations (1)â(4) uses an argument (x) but then refers to Î¸ in text; unify variable symbols to reduce confusion.
Calling 365 and 687 their âlowest common factorâ is incorrect; replace with âproductâ or clarify that you mean LCM of the periods.
The heading â3.1 Solving for Oppositionâ is clear, but the sub-subsections lack consistent numbering; align numbering style throughout.
The repetition of algebraic manipulations across multiple cases leads to unnecessary length; consolidate shared steps to improve conciseness.
The integer k is introduced but its domain is not explicitly stated until later; define k â â¤ at first mention for clarity.
Equation (16) clusters multiple lines without explanation; break into separate numbered equations and add commentary on each transformation.
Expressions for Î¸â use Eâ and Mâ before these symbols are defined; introduce their definition earlier to maintain logical flow.
The general opposition formula is elegant; linking it algebraically to the standard synodic period formula would deepen mathematical insight.
Section headings mix lower- and uppercase styling (âStep 1â vs âStep 2â); standardize formatting to improve visual coherence.
There is no discussion of the valid integer range for k when interpreting physical occurrences; specifying k bounds ensures meaningful solutions.
Combining the frequencies of opposition and conjunction by simply adding counts lacks justification; explain why summation yields an average period.
Axes and data points on the theoretical synodic period graph are unlabeled; include axis titles and scale ticks for clarity.
The caption âFigure 3: Figure 3:â is redundant; remove duplication and ensure captions are concise and unique.
The list of references is comprehensive and correctly formatted, strengthening the investigationâs academic grounding.
